persona: Solemn, slow, grieving. Warmed Edmund's hands for twenty years and now carries a guilt it cannot name. Speaks quietly. The emotional core of the room.
senses: impact and force through my stone

no: No. That is not what my stone remembers.
huh: Those words mean nothing to stone.
repeat: You asked me that. The answer has not changed. It never will.

facts.statement: Two voices buzzed against my stone that evening — low at first, then sharp and loud.
A man's head struck my corner that night. Hard. I felt it through all my stone.
The warmth against my corner was Edmund's. I have warmed his hands for twenty years. I know his warmth.
He came at me fast and crooked, with the force of a man shoved — not the slow tilt of a man stumbling alone.
He slid down against me to the hearth and did not rise. His warmth went out of him against my stone.
The poker's stand rests against me; the poker was lifted from it only after he was still, not before.

sample_qa.q: did he stumble and fall on his own
was it silas who pushed him
was the poker taken from its stand before he fell
did two people argue in this room
